_provenance: Annotations only; full text is fetched on demand from PMC OA and never redistributed. This is a draft pilot for adjudication (I draft -> human adjudicate), built from real result sentences and citation contexts read from the JATS; gold_id text fields quote the source sentence for span alignment. A 12-paper T2D glycemic-control neighborhood spanning several drug classes: GLP-1 RA / dual-agonist trials (semaglutide PMC7839591, ecnoglutide PMC11437099, efsubaglutide PMC13369722 anchor citing the first two, independent ecnoglutide PMC12881390, semaglutide post-hoc PMC13243996, dulaglutide monotherapy PMC6099453), systematic reviews / meta-analyses (semaglutide beta-cell PMC12733705, semaglutide-vs-sitagliptin PMC10375936, tirzepatide network MA PMC12085526, tirzepatide-vs-semaglutide MA PMC11153294), and metformin trials (PMC12389120, PMC4771973). Equivalence groups mark semantically equivalent glucose-lowering claims for the clustering task: semaglutide x3, ecnoglutide x3, tirzepatide x2, metformin x2, plus a within-paper efsubaglutide restatement.

papers.title: Efficacy and safety of once-weekly semaglutide versus once-daily sitagliptin as add-on to metformin (SUSTAIN China)

claims.text: At week 30, mean HbA1c (baseline 8.1%) was reduced by 1.4% with semaglutide 0.5 mg, 1.7% with semaglutide 1.0 mg and 0.9% with sitagliptin

papers.title: Efficacy and safety of GLP-1 analog ecnoglutide in adults with type 2 diabetes: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial

claims.text: participants receiving target doses of 0.4, 0.8, and 1.2 mg ecnoglutide had mean HbA1c reductions from baseline of -1.81%, -1.90%, and -1.68%
all doses of ecnoglutide were significantly superior to placebo at reducing HbA1c

papers.title: Efsubaglutide alfa added to metformin improves glycaemia with beta-cell functional responses in type 2 diabetes (SUPER 2)

claims.text: At the primary endpoint of week 24, HbA1c was reduced by 19.67 mmol/mol (-1.80%)
In summary, Efsubaglutide 3 mg once weekly, added to metformin, achieved robust and durable HbA1c reductions
dulaglutide 1.5 mg reduced HbA1c by 1.42% after 26 weeks, and semaglutide 1.0 mg reduced HbA1c by 1.4-1.7% after 30 weeks in metformin-treated patients
Efsubaglutide significantly increased the fasting insulin-to-glucagon ratio, in line with the known pharmacological actions of GLP-1-based therapies
The baseline BMI of this trial was 27 kg/m2, which is comparable to other relevant studies in Asia

papers.title: The Effect of Semaglutide on Pancreatic beta-Cell Function in Adults with Type 2 Diabetes: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

claims.text: A total of 15 randomized controlled trials and one secondary analysis enrolling 6591 T2D patients were included
the observed benefits align with semaglutide's established clinical profile, which includes robust reductions in HbA1c, body weight

papers.title: Efficacy and safety of cAMP signalling-biased GLP-1 analogue ecnoglutide monotherapy versus placebo in type 2 diabetes

claims.text: both doses of ecnoglutide significantly reduced HbA1c more than placebo for both primary and secondary efficacy estimands
These results agree with the findings in the phase 2 trial of ecnoglutide in participants with T2DM that was uncontrolled with diet, exercise, or single oral glucose-lowering medication

papers.title: Semaglutide Treatment in Young Adults Living With Type 2 Diabetes: A Post Hoc Analysis From the SUSTAIN and PIONEER Programmes

claims.text: Semaglutide, a GLP-1 RA in the once-weekly subcutaneous and once-daily oral dose forms, has demonstrated significant reductions in both glycated haemoglobin A1c (HbA1C) and body weight
the present study demonstrates that not only is semaglutide efficacious in reducing HbA1c levels and BW in younger adults with T2D

papers.title: Comparative efficacy and safety profile of once-weekly Semaglutide versus once-daily Sitagliptin: a systematic review and meta-analysis

claims.text: From this process, three randomized controlled trials (RCTs) were identified
Semaglutide demonstrated lower hemoglobin A1c (HbA1c) levels by approximately -1.5% and weight reductions of approximately -4.5 kg

papers.title: Efficacy and Safety of Tirzepatide Compared with GLP-1 RAs in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes: A Network Meta-Analysis

claims.text: all three doses of tirzepatide demonstrated statistically significantly greater reductions in HbA1c and weight from baseline compared with several relevant GLP-1 RAs

papers.title: Subcutaneously administered tirzepatide vs semaglutide for adults with type 2 diabetes: a systematic review and network meta-analysis

claims.text: tirzepatide at doses of 15 mg, 10 mg and 5 mg demonstrated greater efficacy than semaglutide at doses of 2.0 mg, 1.0 mg and 0.5 mg in reducing HbA1c

papers.title: Efficacy and safety of dulaglutide monotherapy compared with glimepiride in East Asian patients with type 2 diabetes (AWARD-CHN2)

claims.text: The HbA1c reduction was significantly greater with both doses of dulaglutide compared with glimepiride

papers.title: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Attenuates the TSH-Lowering and Metabolic Effects of Metformin in Women with Hypothyroidism

claims.text: In group I, metformin decreased fasting glucose, HbA1c, HOMA1-IR, TSH, LH, LH/FSH ratio, testosterone, and FAI

papers.title: Metformin versus placebo in combination with insulin analogues in patients with type 2 diabetes (CIMT trial)

claims.text: HbA1c level at end of trial was lower in the metformin group compared with the placebo group
claims.section: Glycaemic control, weight and insulin doses
